summary: Reclassified the optional physical-device and TestFlight localization proof tracks from blocked work to deferred manual-extension slices. Their entry conditions now require human-provided external evidence with build provenance before reopening, while docs and the HIG matrix manifest explicitly preserve the existing policy that repo-managed simulator proof is sufficient for the `hig-ui-reviewed` claim and cannot be upgraded to `device-verified` or `testflight-verified`.

contract_status_changes.before: Optional physical-device and TestFlight localization proof slices appeared as blocked work, even though the HIG UI claim policy already accepted repo-managed simulator proof for `hig-ui-reviewed`.
contract_status_changes.after: The optional external proof slices are deferred manual-extension tracks and re-open only when human-provided device/TestFlight evidence exists; automated simulator proof remains the accepted HIG UI bar and does not imply device/testflight proof.

residual_risks: Physical-device and TestFlight localization proof remain unclaimed by design; they require external human-provided evidence before reopening.
recommended_next_reason: After external proof tracks are deferred, the remaining automatable localization unblocker is deterministic iPhone SE simulator provisioning for the smallest-width regression slice.
